image_dependency: Question explicitly says the classes are 'as shown in the figure'.
The labels I/II/III are not defined in text, so the figure is needed to map branching classes to the answer order.
multi_step: Solving requires identifying which displayed class is more or less branched, then applying the chemistry rule relating branching to boiling point.
The final answer is an ordering across three classes rather than a direct lookup.
Multiple-choice format limits complexity, so the reasoning burden appears moderate rather than high.
verifiability: Answer is constrained and clear: `answer_text` is `III > II > I`, and it matches one of the provided choices.
The underlying rule is standard and checkable: among hexane isomers, increased branching lowers boiling point.
Asset registry passed (`registry_passed=true`), and both question and answer are present.
